sections.name: Standard purchased parts
rules: Screws: crop the screw body at the item callout target; avoid adjacent washers unless the washer is the requested row.
Nuts: crop the nut body at the item callout target; avoid the entire fastener stack.
Washers: crop the ring/washer body at the item callout target; do not reuse the screw crop.
Rivet nuts: crop the visible rivet nut cylinder or ring, preferably from an isometric or exploded view.
O-rings: crop the marked O-ring target from the item callout. If represented only as a small dot or ring in an exploded view, crop that target with minimal surrounding part context.

sections.name: Sheet metal and assemblies
rules: Sheet-metal child rows may use the smallest isometric body, flat pattern, or main part view that identifies the child part.
Assembly rows may use an exploded or overall geometry view, but child rows should not reuse the assembly crop.
Seal/profile rows should crop the actual profile body or edge-protection strip, not notes about bonding or installation.
